description: Defines allowed responsibilities and dependencies between frontend, routes, mappers, services, repositories, and database.

rules.name: Routes delegate business work
rules.description: Route handlers must handle HTTP concerns, validation, mapping, and response sending, then delegate use cases to services.

rules.name: Services orchestrate only
rules.description: Services must coordinate validators, repositories, and domain operations without SQL, raw HTTP payload parsing, or response dictionary construction.

rules.name: Repositories own persistence
rules.description: Repositories must own SQL/database access and return domain objects, DTOs, booleans, or repository-level results.

rules.name: Mappers own conversion
rules.description: Mappers must own conversion between API payloads, DTOs, domain objects, database rows, and response dictionaries.

rules.name: No upward dependency leakage
rules.description: Lower layers must not depend on frontend, HTTP handlers, route utilities, or UI field names.

rules.name: Frontend must not bypass backend architecture
rules.description: Frontend code must communicate through approved API/service boundaries and must not directly access persistence, infrastructure, or internal backend implementation details.
